mnemonics: Please Do Not Throw Sausage Pizza Away (for OSI layers: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application)
All People Seem To Need Data Processing (alternate for OSI layers: Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical)
TCP/IP Suite: AT-N-I (Application, Transport, Internet, Network Interface)
keyPoints: The OSI model is a 7-layer conceptual framework, while the TCP/IP model is a 4-layer practical implementation.
The Network Layer (Layer 3) is responsible for logical addressing (IP) and routing.
The Transport Layer (Layer 4) is responsible for end-to-end connections and reliability (TCP) or speed (UDP).
commonMistakes: Confusing the OSI and TCP/IP models and mapping their layers incorrectly (e.g., thinking TCP is Layer 3).
Misunderstanding the core function of each layer (e.g., thinking routers work at Layer 2).
Assuming all network communication requires a connection-oriented protocol like TCP, ignoring the role of UDP.
